---
date: 2025/05/15
date-format: long
date-modified: last-modified
---

# BIODYNAMICS - Vegetation Co-occurrence

This is a project to study the co-occurrence of vegetation across space and time. 

The repo is part of the [BIODYNAMICS](https://bit.ly/BIODYNAMICS) project.

![](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-WIP-red) This project is a work in progress. The code is not yet ready!

## Current Status

The project is using {[config](https://rstudio.github.io/config/index.html)} to set up various "subprojects" within the main project. This allos to have a small subset of the data to run quick tests and examples, while the main project is still being developed.

The main source of data is [VegVault](bit.ly/VegVault), a database of vegetation plots and abiotic data. 

Currently, the aim of the project is to estimate the number of significant co-occurrences of species in a given area per each time slice (step). The species associations are calculated using {[sjSDM](https://theoreticalecology.github.io/s-jSDM/)} (Scalable Joint Species Distribution Models) and the number of significant co-occurrences is calculated as residual co-occurrences (after constraining for abiotic variables).

### Framework migration: HMSC → sjSDM

The project was originally developed using {[HMSC](https://www.helsinki.fi/en/researchgroups/statistical-ecology/software/hmsc)} (Hierarchical Modelling of Species Communities). However, HMSC relies on MCMC sampling, which did not scale well to the size of the data used in this project — fitting times became prohibitively long as the number of sites, taxa, and time slices increased. The project is therefore being **fully reworked to use {[sjSDM](https://theoreticalecology.github.io/s-jSDM/)}**, which uses stochastic gradient descent and GPU acceleration to achieve substantial performance gains while retaining the joint species distribution modelling framework.

> **⚠️ TO BE DONE:** The **spatial random effect** component has not yet been implemented in the new sjSDM pipeline. Accounting for spatial autocorrelation is a planned next step and will be added once the core abiotic-constrained association pipeline is stable.

Here is a preview of the current status of the project status:

<iframe src="/Documentation/Progress/project_status.html" width="100%" height="500px" style="border:none;"></iframe>

Or you can see the simplified static version of the project status below:

![](/Documentation/Progress/project_status_static.png)

### Preliminary Results

> **🔄 In progress:** The pipeline has been migrated from {HMSC} to {[sjSDM](https://theoreticalecology.github.io/s-jSDM/)} and is actively being developed. Results below reflect the current state of the new pipeline and will be updated as the workflow stabilises.

There is a toy sub-project with the area of Czech Republic using only the fossil pollen archive data spanning 0–5,000 cal yr BP, with a time step of 500 years. The abiotic variables are bio1 and bio12 (mean annual temperature and annual precipitation). Only the 10 most common taxa are used in the analysis. The sjSDM model is fitted with linear abiotic effects and no explicit spatial random effect.

```{r}
#| label: plot-species-associations
#| echo: false
#| output: true
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| error: false
suppressMessages(
  suppressWarnings({
    library(targets, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
    library(here, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
    here::i_am("website/about.qmd")
  })
)

set_store <-
  paste0(
    config::get(
      value = "target_store",
      config = "project_cz",
      use_parent = FALSE,
      file = here::here("config.yml")
    ),
    "/", "pipeline_basic", "/"
  ) |>
  here::here()

targets::tar_read(
  name = "plot_species_associations",
  store = set_store
)
```
